description_long: This is a wooden picket fence section composed of six vertical slats evenly spaced and connected by two horizontal rails at the top and bottom. The wood appears to be smooth and finished, with a light tan color that suggests it is either pine or a similar softwood. Each end of the fence features squared posts that extend slightly above the top rail, providing a simple but sturdy structure. The top edge of the fence is flat, with no decorative elements or variations in height. The construction is solid and utilitarian, suitable for marking boundaries or enclosing small areas in urban parks, gardens, or residential yards. There are no gates, hinges, or interactive components visible, emphasizing its function as a straightforward barrier.
description: A light tan wooden picket fence with six vertical slats and two horizontal rails, featuring a flat top and squared posts. The design is simple, sturdy, and utilitarian, with no decorative elements.
description_view_0: Front view showing six evenly spaced vertical slats, two horizontal rails, and squared posts at each end.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the fence, with the vertical slats and rails aligned in a single plane.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the same arrangement of slats and rails, with no additional features.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the narrow profile and the alignment of the slats and rails.
